message:
fix(runtime/browser): non-clobbering addEventListener-based message and error listeners

The browser reactor previously used set_onmessage()/set_onmessageerror()
which clobber any existing handlers on the target.  This is unsafe in
shared-port scenarios where other code may also need message events.

Fix: New attach_browser_message_listeners() and
detach_browser_message_listeners() helpers use the standard
EventTarget.addEventListener()/removeEventListener() API with named
event constants (BROWSER_MESSAGE_EVENT, BROWSER_MESSAGE_ERROR_EVENT).
This is non-clobbering — multiple listeners can coexist on the same
target.

Both MessagePort and BroadcastChannel bindings now:
- Cast to EventTarget and use the add/remove listener helpers
- Return io::Result from attach() (was unit), propagating JsValue
  errors via browser_host_listener_error()
- Import JsValue and use JsCast for the EventTarget upcast

Doc comments updated to say 'non-clobbering listeners' instead of
'claims onmessage handlers'.  Test formatting cleaned up (rustfmt).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
